summary:
I have investigated the issue and found that the circular import was occurring because `main.py` imports `users.py`, which in turn imports `notifications.py`. During the module initialization of `notifications.py`, it was attempting to access `users.DEFAULT_ROLE` before `users.py` had finished initializing it. 

To resolve the circular import without breaking functionality, I modified `notifications.py` to evaluate `users.DEFAULT_ROLE` at runtime inside the `send_welcome_email` method instead of storing it as a global variable.

I have executed `./test.sh` and successfully verified that all startup and functionality tests now pass.
